title: Ash-Sigil Summons
body: A courier presses a hot sigil into your palm; it beats in time with the Fracture Choir beneath Ember Hollow.
If the Crownfire knot is left unattended, shrine wards will fail and Gloamreach will burn from within.

choices.text: Report to Thorn Bastion for command briefing
Work Cinder Market for leverage before reporting

lore: The Ember knot is one side of the same Fracture Choir destabilizing both regions.

title: Council Under Cinders
body: Thorn Bastion commanders argue whether to seal the tunnels or trust an outside atlas-bearer with the descent.
Your decision sets who controls the Crownfire response: discipline, improvisation, or fear.

choices.text: Swear Concord custody over the Crownfire response
Run an unsanctioned smokeline breach with local guides

lore: Concord claims emergency authority over Crownfire rites when shrine wards fail.

title: Oathbound Procession
body: Concord wardens and pilgrim chanters march mirrored braziers toward Ashen Gate beneath your sworn command.
If formation breaks, the Regent below wakes fully and the oath you swore becomes public failure.

choices.text: Hold ritual formation and keep braziers lit

success.text: You hold formation and the Choir pulse stays stable.

mixed.text: You keep the line moving, but the smoke brands your lungs.

failForward.text: The route collapses behind you; only a forward breach remains.

effects.message: Fail-forward: the story surges toward the gate.

choices.text: Invoke Bastion authority and force a fast, costly advance

lore: Mirrored braziers buffer Choir pulses long enough to open a controlled breach.

title: Smokeline Breach
body: Smuggler-guides thread you through collapsed shrine lanes where Bastion banners never held.
If you misread the smoke currents, the Regent wakes before any formal breach line can form.

choices.text: Coordinate covert braziers through undercourt alleys

success.text: Your covert route reaches Ashen Gate before Concord scouts can react.

mixed.text: You reach the gate, but half the line arrives injured and furious.

failForward.text: A tunnel collapse isolates your rear guard, but your vanguard still reaches the gate.

effects.message: Fail-forward: your unsanctioned route burns goodwill, not momentum.

choices.text: Sprint the last span and accept brutal losses

lore: Smokeline routes bypass formal command, trading discipline for speed.

title: Mouth of the Embervault
body: Ashen Gate opens like a furnace lung while mural saints depict the first oath that failed this region.
Every delayed minute amplifies Choir pressure through nearby shrines and camps.

choices.text: Breach now and sever the Crownfire knot
Delay once to evacuate stragglers and brace the outer wards

effects.message: Delay buys evacuations but the knot surges harder.

lore: Ashen Gate murals record the first treaty that failed to contain Crownfire.

title: Crownfire Surge Window
body: Evacuation horns fade as the gate shudders; you bought lives, but pressure spikes through every wardline.
There is no safe stall left. The next move decides whether this delay becomes mercy or catastrophe.

choices.text: Commit the breach now under surge conditions
Dispatch emergency caravans through Cinder Market first

lore: Delays convert tactical risk into civic debt; someone always pays for extra time.

title: Verdict of Crownfire
body: The Ash Regent kneels as the Ember Crown sings in molten harmonics that can heal, dominate, or bind.
Your final rite will set Gloamreach policy for every faction watching the breach reports.

choices.text: Diffuse Crownfire into rain for civilian relief
Crown yourself and centralize command by fear
Chain Crownfire to Bastion treaty law

lore: Who governs Crownfire doctrine determines border law, trade, and military legitimacy.

title: Dawn Mercy Accord
body: Steam turns to rain and shrine bells resume a human tempo across Ember Hollow.
Pilgrims name this ending a mercy: power returned to the commons, not a throne.

choices.text: Canonize the Dawn Mercy precedent

lore: The Dawn doctrine returns heat-rights to civilian governance.

title: Regency of Ash
body: You wear the Crown and every firepost bows to your cadence before dissent can gather.
Order arrives quickly, but it is obedience shaped by fear of your heat-song.

choices.text: Formalize the Ash Regency order

lore: The Ash doctrine centralizes force under one command hierarchy.

title: Iron Binding Charter
body: The Crown is chained beneath Thorn Bastion by sigils that require endless maintenance and tribute.
Stability holds, but political debt deepens as Concord and pilgrims renegotiate authority.

choices.text: Enact the Iron Binding charter

lore: The Oath doctrine binds energy access to treaty enforcement.

title: Hollow Aftermath
body: The oathstones cool, yet your atlas still hums; the Fracture Choir was damped here, not ended.
New harmonics rise from the glass desert, where the Starwell repeats names from the dead.

choices.text: Follow the new harmonic toward Prism Steppe
Log the aftermath and return to atlas

lore: The Ember knot is stabilized, but the Steppe knot now resonates louder.

title: Glasswake Summons
body: Beneath Prism Steppe, a Wellsong pulse answers the Crownfire cadence you already survived in Gloamreach.
If unchecked, the second knot will rewrite routes, memories, and command signals across both regions.

choices.text: Track the Wellsong to Sunken Orrery
Investigate Mirror Lake before committing

lore: The Wellsong knot echoes every policy chosen at Ember Hollow.

title: Orrery Breach
body: Collapsed planets spin backward inside the Orrery, projecting a map that points to the Starwell rim.
Decode quickly and you control the approach; hesitate and rivals seize the route.

choices.text: Decode with disciplined harmonic math

success.text: Your calculations align the route with minimal drift.

mixed.text: You recover a viable route, but trigger defensive alarms.

failForward.text: A collapse blocks retreat and forces a dangerous shortcut.

choices.text: Decode by instinct and tactile memory

lore: Orrery routes decide which faction reaches the Starwell first.

title: Judgment at Eclipse Court
body: Obsidian jurors offer expedited passage if you surrender memory to their archive of leverage.
Refusal preserves self-rule but costs time while the Wellsong grows louder.

choices.text: Trade memory for sanctioned passage
Refuse tribute and cross the starstorm

lore: The Eclipse Court exchanges memory debt for strategic mobility.

title: Rim of the Starwell
body: Constellations spiral into a black throat; each cycle syncs with echoes from Ember Hollow.
This descent decides whether the Fracture Choir is tuned, caged, or weaponized.

choices.text: Descend and confront the Wellsong source
Delay for calmer skies and lower risk

lore: The Starwell descent resolves whether the sky-knot is shared, chained, or weaponized.

title: Seraph Harmonic Verdict
body: The Wellsong Seraph offers three harmonics that can calm, shroud, or politically bind the sky-song.
Your ruling will set cross-region doctrine for pilgrims, Concord officers, and the Undercourt.

choices.text: Stabilize the sky into a calm tide
Impose controlled night over the regions
Bind factions to one enforced chorus

lore: This verdict sets long-term doctrine across both regions.

title: Tide Accord
body: Star paths settle and caravans report predictable skies for the first time since the fracture.
The accord favors shared stewardship and lowers panic across both regions.

choices.text: Seal the Tide Accord record

lore: Tide doctrine restores predictable sky lanes and shared transit rights.

title: Veil Directorate
body: You drape the sky in controlled twilight and centralize access to navigable corridors.
Security improves, but dissent spreads among those locked out of light.

choices.text: Seal the Veil Directorate order

lore: Veil doctrine secures order through restricted light corridors.

title: Chorus Compact
body: Every faction hears the same oath at dawn and policy converges under a mandatory shared rhythm.
War recedes, yet autonomy narrows as dissent is treated as dissonance.

choices.text: Ratify the Chorus Compact covenant

lore: Union doctrine enforces consensus through harmonized oath law.

title: Steppe Aftermath
body: The glass plains hold steady tempo, and reports confirm both crisis knots now answer your prior rulings.
Future runs will test alternate doctrines, but this campaign closes with a coherent atlas record.

choices.text: Archive this campaign and return to atlas

title: Cinder Market Briefing
body: Brokers auction route rumors and Choir telemetry while faction buyers posture around smoky ledgers.
Whatever you purchase here changes who reaches the next crisis front first.

choices.text: Shadow a broker into restricted stalls
Bid on encrypted crisis route data

success.text: You outbid rivals and secure verified route timings.

mixed.text: You win partial data and owe a dangerous favor.

failForward.text: You lose the bid, but steal enough clues while exiting.

choices.text: Withdraw before terms harden against you

lore: Market intelligence here influences who controls next-turn deployments.

title: Root Archive Vestibule
body: The living archive catalogs every prior Fracture Choir cycle and who profited from each collapse.
Your next move determines whether knowledge stays civic, military, or black-market.

choices.text: Descend for sealed crisis annals
Negotiate with the root curator
Close your folio and leave quietly

lore: Archive records can legitimize reform or weaponize selective history.

title: Mire Crossing Under Pressure
body: Acid fog rises as bridge crews rush supplies meant for Choir containment lines upstream.
Crossing decisions here decide whether civilians get aid before the next surge.

choices.text: Commit to the bridge and test anchors
Drop to the shrine for emergency stores

lore: Supply lanes through Mire Bridge keep relief and ritual teams operational.

title: Mirror Lake Diagnostics
body: The lake shows alternate responses to the same crisis, each with different faction casualties.
Step through for hard foresight, or leave before the reflection edits your certainty.

choices.text: Enter the reflection to audit futures
Reject the vision and preserve present judgment

lore: The lake models branching outcomes from your campaign doctrines.

title: Shard Forge Mobilization
body: Forge crews repair sky compasses needed to route caravans around new Choir pressure fronts.
Helping now determines which faction controls navigation during the next crisis week.

choices.text: Join the line and pay in blood
Negotiate terms and fund repairs with mana

lore: Compass output controls which corridors remain navigable under pressure.

title: Backroom Negotiation
body: Smugglers move heatproof gear and intercepted dispatches from both arc theaters in one room.
Your handling of this exchange sets trust and rumor flow for upcoming fronts.

choices.text: Purchase gear and lock in smuggler trust
Pressure the courier for route names

success.text: The courier breaks and names gate schedules in full.

mixed.text: You get fragments, but earn a lasting grudge.

failForward.text: Steel flashes, yet the satchel opens during the struggle.

choices.text: Disengage before leverage flips on you

lore: Backroom logistics convert rumor into operational leverage.

title: Route Ledger Settlement
body: Final signatures decide whether evacuation lanes favor pilgrim caravans or Undercourt buyers.
This is logistics warfare disguised as commerce.

choices.text: Fund civilian caravans and stabilize routes
Sell route priority to the Undercourt

lore: Route funding policy determines civilian access versus toll extraction.

title: Deepstack Retrieval
body: Charred vellum pits contain records linking the Regent lineage to the Wellsong custodians.
Recovering them can legitimize reform or arm propagandists.

choices.text: Study sealed annals despite spore risk

success.text: You reconstruct the lineage with precise archival control.

mixed.text: You secure truth, but leave with toxic lung scars.

failForward.text: Stacks collapse, yet one critical scroll survives in hand.

choices.text: Salvage practical tools for field teams

lore: Deepstack annals tie Regent and Wellsong custodians to prior collapses.

title: Living Index Decision
body: The index rewrites in real time as factions submit claims to historical legitimacy.
Where you publish these records changes command trust across both regions.

choices.text: Release records to pilgrim councils
Sell curated pages to Concord command

lore: Publication strategy shapes trust in every faction command chain.

title: Midspan Gale
body: Bridge cables scream as supply crates swing over acid mist and panicked workers lose cadence.
A clean crossing keeps the containment line provisioned before nightfall.

choices.text: Dash the final span before cable snap

success.text: You cross cleanly and mark a safe emergency lane.

mixed.text: You make it across, but inhale burning vapor.

failForward.text: You slip hard, recover, and finish the route bleeding.

lore: A failed crossing delays containment convoys and increases local pressure.

title: Sunken Relief Cache
body: A sealed chest under the shrine holds rope kits and stimulant salts for bridge crews.
Take supplies for yourself or leave them for wider relief.

choices.text: Open the chest and claim emergency gear
Leave supplies and secure the route marker

lore: Cache allocation directly affects district survival odds.

title: Underface Current
body: Below the surface, alternate timelines argue over your prior rulings in Ember Hollow and the Steppe.
Accepting insight grants precision but increases psychological strain.

choices.text: Accept the painful forecast and remember it

success.text: You surface with a clear and actionable forecast.

mixed.text: You keep the omen, but it leaves a lasting migraine.

failForward.text: The vision fractures your confidence, yet reveals one key warning.

choices.text: Abort the dive and keep your judgment intact

lore: Forecasts trade psychological strain for strategic foresight.

title: Wake of Splintered Futures
body: Your reflection lags by a heartbeat while scouts ask whether to act on what you learned.
Sharing or hoarding the omen changes who trusts your crisis doctrine.

choices.text: Brief Concord scouts on the omen
Keep the omen private and equip quietly

lore: Disclosing prophecy shifts institutional trust and response tempo.

title: Quench Line
body: Molten shard batches cool in sequence while foremen shout timings tied to evacuation windows.
One precise run can restore navigation capacity for an entire district.

choices.text: Take precision hammer duty at the quench

success.text: Your strike cadence stabilizes the full production run.

mixed.text: The batch survives, but your hands blister badly.

failForward.text: A spill burns the floor, yet you salvage enough output.

lore: One stable production run can reopen multiple relief routes.

title: Commission Routing
body: The repaired compass can guide pilgrim relief columns or privileged Undercourt convoys.
This dispatch determines who moves first when pressure spikes again.

choices.text: Route the compass to pilgrim caravans
Sell first-route rights to the Undercourt

lore: Final recipient determines whether routing becomes public utility or private privilege.
